  "findings": [
    "Soft foot condition left uncorrected prior to final shaft alignment",
    "Dial indicator reading exceeds allowable soft foot tolerance"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Loosen hold-down bolts and measure individual foot deflection using dial indicator or laser system",
    "Add or adjust precision shims under the affected foot to eliminate soft foot",
    "Re-torque hold-down bolts to specification and re-verify alignment"
  ],
  "rationale": "The shaft alignment was roughed in but a soft foot condition remains uncorrected. According to API 686, soft foot must be corrected to within 0.05 mm (0.002 inches) or less before final alignment can proceed. Leaving a soft foot uncorrected introduces frame distortion, high vibration, and premature bearing failure when hold-down bolts are torqued.",
  "references": [
    "API RP 686 Chapter 7 (Shaft Alignment)",
    "Laser Alignment System Manuals (Soft Foot Check Procedure)"
  ]
}
